faq.question: What does Ruma Rooftop help with?
faq.answer: Ruma Rooftop can present its core Restaurant services clearly here, with final wording replaced by owner-approved details.

faq.question: How do customers get started?

faq.question: Is this content final?
faq.answer: Demo supporting copy can be AI-completed, while contact facts, claims, pricing, and legal/medical details must be verified before launch.

questionnaireAnswers.primarySurface: Responsive - all sizes, with mobile-first contact actions

findings.title: Phone path is not obvious enough
findings.evidence: The captured page text/link data does not show a clear phone action.
findings.impact: Field-service visitors often want the fastest quote path; hiding the phone path can leak high-intent enquiries.
findings.fix: Make call/quote actions persistent in the hero and mobile header.

findings.title: Core services are not instantly clear
findings.evidence: Expected services were not all visible in captured text: Restaurant.
findings.impact: A lead may not recognize that the business solves their specific problem.
findings.fix: Show 3-5 core services near the top with plain-language outcomes.

findings.title: Page is hard to scan
findings.evidence: Only 2 readable heading(s) were captured.
findings.impact: Skimming visitors have fewer anchors for services, proof, service area, and CTA.
findings.fix: Add clear section headings for services, proof, service area, and final CTA.

findings.title: Trust signal is not strong in the first screen
findings.evidence: The first captured text block does not clearly surface reviews, warranty, credentials, years, or local proof.
findings.impact: For local services, trust often matters before design polish.
findings.fix: Add a compact trust strip near the hero: reviews, warranty/licence claims when verified, service area, and response promise.

findings.title: Proof is not doing enough work
findings.evidence: The captured text does not clearly surface reviews, project proof, warranty, licences, or gallery proof.
findings.impact: The site may feel informational rather than convincing.
findings.fix: Add a proof section with real reviews, project examples, and verified guarantees/credentials.

findings.title: Title does not target the main local intent
findings.evidence: Captured title: "RUMA ROOFTOP". Inferred keyword: "restaurant Restaurant".
findings.impact: The page may rank or click worse for the service/location query we care about.
findings.fix: Rewrite title around "restaurant Restaurant" while keeping the business name.

findings.title: Meta description is missing
findings.evidence: No meta description was captured.
findings.impact: Search snippets may be generic or less compelling.
findings.fix: Write a specific 1-2 sentence snippet with service, location, and next action.

findings.title: H1 misses the main local intent
findings.evidence: Captured H1: "Mother’s Day at Ruma". Inferred keyword: "restaurant Restaurant".
findings.impact: Visitors and search engines may not see the core service immediately.
findings.fix: Make the H1 naturally cover "restaurant Restaurant" or a close variant.

findings.title: Page content is thin
findings.evidence: 208 words captured.
findings.impact: Thin pages have fewer chances to explain services, locations, and trust.
findings.fix: Add concise service, proof, area, and FAQ copy without stuffing.

findings.title: Social proof channels are missing
findings.evidence: No visible Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, YouTube, X, or WhatsApp link was captured.
findings.impact: Visual or review-heavy businesses lose an easy trust path.
findings.fix: Add verified social links or replace with stronger on-page testimonials/gallery proof.

findings.title: Basic trust/legal links are thin
findings.evidence: Privacy or terms links were not captured from visible page links.
findings.impact: This is a minor trust and SEO hygiene gap, especially when forms collect customer data.
findings.fix: Add privacy and terms links in the footer when forms or tracking are used.

findings.title: Social sharing tags are incomplete
findings.evidence: OG title: true, description: false, image: true.
findings.impact: Shared previews may look weak when sent in email, SMS, or social DMs.
findings.fix: Add og:title, og:description, and og:image that match the redesigned positioning.

priorityActions.title: Phone path is not obvious enough
priorityActions.fix: Make call/quote actions persistent in the hero and mobile header.

priorityActions.title: Core services are not instantly clear
priorityActions.fix: Show 3-5 core services near the top with plain-language outcomes.

priorityActions.title: Page is hard to scan
priorityActions.fix: Add clear section headings for services, proof, service area, and final CTA.

priorityActions.title: Trust signal is not strong in the first screen
priorityActions.fix: Add a compact trust strip near the hero: reviews, warranty/licence claims when verified, service area, and response promise.

priorityActions.title: Proof is not doing enough work
priorityActions.fix: Add a proof section with real reviews, project examples, and verified guarantees/credentials.

reasons: No external evidence pack yet; using intake and inferred content only.

openDesignHandoffDraft.prompt:
Do not ask follow-up questions. Make reasonable assumptions from the payload and build the concept files now.
This is a pre-sale mockup, so complete the page with internally safe demo copy instead of waiting for missing minor details.

Build a one-page website for Ruma Rooftop, a Restaurant in Brisbane.

Audience: Restaurant presented with a cleaner first impression, faster trust, and a stronger path to enquiry.
Hero angle: Ruma Rooftop deserves a clearer Restaurant presence online
Primary CTA: Get in touch
Visible contact detail: 0467 951 948.
All call buttons must use href="tel:0467951948" and visible text should include 0467 951 948.
Conversion goal: contact form lead; include a contact form routed through the existing Resend transactional email flow to hi@profitslocal.com.
Questionnaire answers: if your design workflow expects a questionnaire, use these answers automatically:
- Primary surface: Responsive - all sizes, mobile-first contact actions.
- Audience: Restaurant presented with a cleaner first impression, faster trust, and a stronger path to enquiry.
- Visual tone: clear, human, locally credible
- Brand context: Pick a tasteful industry-appropriate direction; do not wait for a brand spec.
- CTA contact detail: 0467 951 948.
- Contact form fields: Name, Phone, Service needed, Message, Preferred call time.
Current site audit: 35/100. Use the audit findings to show clear uplift; if the site is already strong, keep the concept restrained.

Sections/pages to cover: hero, menu-or-offer, trust, about, cta.
Services to show: Restaurant, Signature offer, Private bookings, Visit information.
Trust signals: Google profile available, Before/after redesign clarity.

Use tasteful, conversion-focused design. Fill demo copy completely using common-sense industry best practices where verified details are missing.
Create the actual HTML/CSS/JS artifact. Do not return a question form, questionnaire, plan-only response, or request for more input.
Do not invent phone, email, address, awards, prices, certifications, medical/legal claims, or review quotes.
Avoid AI-looking gradients, generic stock photos, empty placeholder blocks, "Welcome to" headlines, and "Your trusted partner" copy.

buildPacket.nextAction: Use the autonomous website handoff for Open Design mockup generation, then replace demo-only content before production launch.

outreachBrief.diagnosis: Ruma Rooftop already has a website, but it likely undersells the real business. The redesign opportunity is to improve clarity, trust, and the path from interest to booking or enquiry. Make the current site feel more credible than the existing version. Make the offer easier to understand at a glance. The biggest leak is usually unclear positioning and a weak next step.
outreachBrief.siteBrief: Ruma Rooftop is a Restaurant in Brisbane. Lead with Ruma Rooftop already has real-world traction; the website should finally feel as strong as the service. Highlight Restaurant. Keep the tone confident, refined, locally credible. The main CTA should be Get in touch. One design choice that should stand out is editorial photography.
outreachBrief.coldMessage: Hey Ruma, I looked at Ruma Rooftop in Brisbane. Maps scraper website status: independent_http_site. I took a look at the current site and mocked up a version that makes the offer clearer and the next step easier for mobile visitors. Happy to send the mockup if you want to see it.
followUps: Quick follow-up: the clearest gap I noticed is hero clarity. I can send over the mockup if it would help.
One more thought: nearby competitors usually do a better job showing CTA path. I already mocked up a tighter version if you want to compare it.
subjectLines: Saw your listing and made this for Ruma Rooftop
